response.justification: Maria Rodriguez brings roughly 15 years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, and some embedded contexts, but is fundamentally mismatched for this role on multiple hard requirements. Most critically, native-level Japanese proficiency is listed as required and is entirely absent from her profile — her listed languages are English, Romanian, and basic German. Beyond that, her core expertise is TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang for web and full-stack work; C programming appears only in dated, brief contexts (NI LabWindows components, a thesis project). There is no demonstrated experience with embedded networking firmware, LAN switch products, Linux kernel development, or any of the required security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, SNMP, NETCONF).
strengths: ~15 years of software engineering experience across diverse platforms (web, mobile, embedded, low-latency systems) shows strong general technical range
Demonstrated low-level and systems-adjacent work: C/C++ components at National Instruments, high-performance async I/O with DLang at Dunnhumby, and hardware work with robotic arms at Altom
Strong engineering discipline evidenced by consistent use of TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and open-source contributions
concerns: Native-level Japanese proficiency is a non-negotiable requirement and is completely absent — this alone disqualifies the candidate
No experience with embedded networking, LAN switch firmware, or relevant security protocols (MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, Captive Portal, SNMP, NETCONF, or Linux kernel security countermeasures)
C programming for UNIX embedded development is not a demonstrated strength; primary career focus has been TypeScript/Node.js/web stacks with only brief and dated C/C++ exposure

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security protocol experience
